title: PBQ Simulations
description: Practice understanding Performance-Based Question (PBQ) types on the CompTIA Network+ exam including drag-and-drop scenarios, fill-in-the-blank configurations, network diagram simulations, command-line tasks, and strategies for completing PBQs effectively.
starterCode:
# PBQ SIMULATIONS EXERCISE

## Part 1: PBQ Types and Format

1. PBQ stands for Performance-_______ Question, a hands-on simulation on the CompTIA exam.
2. PBQs test _______ skills by requiring candidates to perform tasks rather than select answers.
3. _______ and drop PBQs require placing items into correct categories, locations, or sequences.
4. Network _______ PBQs present a topology where candidates must configure or troubleshoot connections.
5. Command-line _______ PBQs require entering correct commands to diagnose or resolve network issues.
6. PBQs typically appear at the _______ of the exam before multiple-choice questions.
7. Candidates can _______ PBQs and return to them later if they need more time for other questions.

## Part 2: Drag-and-Drop Scenarios

8. Drag-and-drop questions may ask candidates to match network _______ to their correct OSI layers.
9. Cable type matching PBQs require associating cable types with correct _______ and use cases.
10. Port number PBQs ask candidates to match common _______ with their associated port numbers.
11. Device _______ PBQs require placing network devices in the correct position within a topology.
12. Troubleshooting _______ PBQs require ordering steps in the correct methodological sequence.
13. Security _______ matching PBQs associate attack types with appropriate countermeasures.
14. IP addressing PBQs may require matching _______ ranges to their correct network segments.

## Part 3: Network Diagram Simulations

15. Network diagram PBQs may present a _______ with misconfigured devices that need correction.
16. Candidates may need to assign correct IP _______ to devices based on the subnet scheme shown.
17. VLAN _______ PBQs require assigning switch ports to the correct VLANs based on requirements.
18. Routing _______ PBQs may require configuring static routes or identifying routing protocol settings.
19. Firewall _______ PBQs involve creating or modifying access control lists to permit or deny traffic.
20. Wireless _______ PBQs may require configuring SSID, channel, security, and power settings.
21. Cable _______ PBQs ask candidates to select the correct cable type for each connection in a diagram.

## Part 4: Command-Line PBQs

22. Candidates may need to use _______ to verify connectivity to a remote host by IP or hostname.
23. The _______ command traces the path packets take through the network, showing each hop.
24. Using _______ (or ifconfig on Linux) displays the current IP configuration of network adapters.
25. The _______ command queries DNS servers to resolve hostnames to IP addresses.
26. Candidates may need to use _______ to display active connections, listening ports, and routing tables.
27. The arp _______ command displays the ARP cache, showing IP-to-MAC address mappings.
28. PBQs may require using _______ commands to configure interface settings on a switch or router.

## Part 5: PBQ Strategies and Tips

29. Read the entire PBQ _______ carefully before beginning to understand all requirements.
30. PBQs are worth _______ points than standard multiple-choice questions on the exam.
31. Time _______ is critical — don't spend excessive time on a single PBQ at the expense of other questions.
32. If unsure about a PBQ, _______ it and complete all multiple-choice questions first.
33. Network diagram PBQs often include _______ information that is not relevant to the solution.
34. Check your _______ in PBQs carefully — small errors like wrong subnet masks can make answers incorrect.
35. Practice with _______ environments and lab simulators before the exam to build confidence.
36. Some PBQs have multiple correct _______ — focus on meeting the stated requirements.
37. Use the _______ or exhibit information provided in the PBQ as clues to the expected configuration.
solution:
# SOLUTION

## Part 1: PBQ Types and Format

1. PBQ stands for Performance-**Based** Question, a hands-on simulation on the CompTIA exam.
2. PBQs test **practical** skills by requiring candidates to perform tasks rather than select answers.
3. **Drag** and drop PBQs require placing items into correct categories, locations, or sequences.
4. Network **diagram** PBQs present a topology where candidates must configure or troubleshoot connections.
5. Command-line **simulation** PBQs require entering correct commands to diagnose or resolve network issues.
6. PBQs typically appear at the **beginning** of the exam before multiple-choice questions.
7. Candidates can **skip** PBQs and return to them later if they need more time for other questions.

## Part 2: Drag-and-Drop Scenarios

8. Drag-and-drop questions may ask candidates to match network **protocols** to their correct OSI layers.
9. Cable type matching PBQs require associating cable types with correct **connectors** and use cases.
10. Port number PBQs ask candidates to match common **services** with their associated port numbers.
11. Device **placement** PBQs require placing network devices in the correct position within a topology.
12. Troubleshooting **methodology** PBQs require ordering steps in the correct methodological sequence.
13. Security **mitigation** matching PBQs associate attack types with appropriate countermeasures.
14. IP addressing PBQs may require matching **subnet** ranges to their correct network segments.

## Part 3: Network Diagram Simulations

15. Network diagram PBQs may present a **topology** with misconfigured devices that need correction.
16. Candidates may need to assign correct IP **addresses** to devices based on the subnet scheme shown.
17. VLAN **configuration** PBQs require assigning switch ports to the correct VLANs based on requirements.
18. Routing **table** PBQs may require configuring static routes or identifying routing protocol settings.
19. Firewall **rule** PBQs involve creating or modifying access control lists to permit or deny traffic.
20. Wireless **access point** PBQs may require configuring SSID, channel, security, and power settings.
21. Cable **selection** PBQs ask candidates to select the correct cable type for each connection in a diagram.

## Part 4: Command-Line PBQs

22. Candidates may need to use **ping** to verify connectivity to a remote host by IP or hostname.
23. The **traceroute** command traces the path packets take through the network, showing each hop.
24. Using **ipconfig** (or ifconfig on Linux) displays the current IP configuration of network adapters.
25. The **nslookup** command queries DNS servers to resolve hostnames to IP addresses.
26. Candidates may need to use **netstat** to display active connections, listening ports, and routing tables.
27. The arp **-a** command displays the ARP cache, showing IP-to-MAC address mappings.
28. PBQs may require using **CLI** commands to configure interface settings on a switch or router.

## Part 5: PBQ Strategies and Tips

29. Read the entire PBQ **instructions** carefully before beginning to understand all requirements.
30. PBQs are worth **more** points than standard multiple-choice questions on the exam.
31. Time **management** is critical — don't spend excessive time on a single PBQ at the expense of other questions.
32. If unsure about a PBQ, **skip** it and complete all multiple-choice questions first.
33. Network diagram PBQs often include **extraneous** information that is not relevant to the solution.
34. Check your **work** in PBQs carefully — small errors like wrong subnet masks can make answers incorrect.
35. Practice with **virtual** environments and lab simulators before the exam to build confidence.
36. Some PBQs have multiple correct **solutions** — focus on meeting the stated requirements.
37. Use the **scenario** or exhibit information provided in the PBQ as clues to the expected configuration.
hints: PBQ = Performance-Based Question; they test practical hands-on skills in simulated network environments.
PBQs appear at the beginning of the exam and can be skipped to complete multiple-choice questions first.
Drag-and-drop PBQs match protocols to OSI layers, services to ports, and cables to connectors.
Network diagram PBQs require configuring IP addresses, VLANs, routing, firewall rules, or wireless settings.
Command-line PBQs test tools: ping, traceroute, ipconfig, nslookup, netstat, and arp commands.
PBQs are worth more points than multiple-choice; manage time carefully and don't get stuck on one.
Read all PBQ instructions completely before starting; network diagrams may contain extraneous information.
Practice with virtual labs and simulators (Packet Tracer, GNS3) to build confidence with hands-on tasks.
Some PBQs have multiple valid solutions; focus on meeting the specific stated requirements.
Check work carefully in PBQs — small errors in IP addresses, subnet masks, or port numbers cause failures.
